{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Administrative Assistant / Front Desk Officer",
  "description": "Sow Real Estate is a world class luxury brand in the delivery of mass housing and luxury apartments.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Administrative Assistant / Front Desk Officer

Job Location: Lekki Phase 1, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time (Strictly On-site)
Industry: Real Estate

Job Summary


	We are seeking a polished, articulate, and highly organized Administrative Assistant / Front Desk Officer to be the face of our organization.&nbsp;
	You will manage our reception area, welcome clients, handle incoming communications, and provide vital administrative support to ensure smooth day to day office operations.


Key Responsibilities


	Front Desk Management: Open the office, Greet visitors warmly, determine their needs, and direct them to the appropriate person or meeting room.
	Communication: Answer, screen, and forward incoming phone calls while providing accurate information. Manage the company&rsquo;s info email account.
	General Office Administration: Sort and distribute daily mail/deliveries. Maintain office filing systems (both digital and physical)
	Scheduling: Coordinate meetings, manage executive calendars, and schedule appointments for clients or team members.
	Inventory Control: Monitor and order office supplies (stationery, toiletries, pantry items) to ensure the workplace remains fully stocked.
	Client Relations: Maintain a clean, professional, and organized reception area that reflects a premium brand image.


Requirements &amp; Qualifications


	Education: B.Sc. or HND in Secretarial Studies, Business Administration, Mass Communication, or a related discipline.
	Experience: 1 - 3 years of experience as a Front Desk Officer, Receptionist, or Administrative Assistant.
	Tech Skills: Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) and Google Workspace.
	Communication: Exceptional verbal and written English communication skills with a clear, professional speaking voice.
	Interpersonal Skills: Friendly, professional demeanor with excellent problem solving skills and emotional intelligence.
	Proximity: Proximity to place Lekki is a n added advantage(e.g., Ikate, Jakande, Agungi, Marwa, Victoria Island) to ensure punctual daily attendance.





Salary &amp; Benefits


	Salary Range: N120,000 monthly
	Work Schedule: Monday &ndash; Friday (9:00 AM &ndash; 6:00 PM).
	Benefits: Paid time off, health insurance (HMO), and opportunities for career growth.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated CV and a professional cover letter to: officialvictorkomolafe@gmail.com and copy: Hrsowreallag@gmail.com using &quot;Admin Assistant / Front Desk - Lekki&quot; as the subject of the email.",

Administrative Assistant / Front Desk Officer Job at Sow Real Estate Limited




        
    

        
        Sow Real Estate is a world class luxury brand in the delivery of mass housing and luxury apartments.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Administrative Assistant / Front Desk Officer

Job Location: Lekki Phase 1, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time (Strictly On-site)
Industry: Real Estate

Job Summary
     

            
    




	We are seeking a polished, articulate, and highly organized Administrative Assistant / Front Desk Officer to be the face of our organization. 
	You will manage our reception area, welcome clients, handle incoming communications, and provide vital administrative support to ensure smooth day to day office operations.


Key Responsibilities


	Front Desk Management: Open the office, Greet visitors warmly, determine their needs, and direct them to the appropriate person or meeting room.
	Communication: Answer, screen, and forward incoming phone calls while providing accurate information. Manage the company’s info email account.
	General Office Administration: Sort and distribute daily mail/deliveries. Maintain office filing systems (both digital and physical)
	Scheduling: Coordinate meetings, manage executive calendars, and schedule appointments for clients or team members.
	Inventory Control: Monitor and order office supplies (stationery, toiletries, pantry items) to ensure the workplace remains fully stocked.
	Client Relations: Maintain a clean, professional, and organized reception area that reflects a premium brand image.


Requirements & Qualifications


	Education: B.Sc. or HND in Secretarial Studies, Business Administration, Mass Communication, or a related discipline.
	Experience: 1 - 3 years of experience as a Front Desk Officer, Receptionist, or Administrative Assistant.
	Tech Skills: Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) and Google Workspace.
	Communication: Exceptional verbal and written English communication skills with a clear, professional speaking voice.
	Interpersonal Skills: Friendly, professional demeanor with excellent problem solving skills and emotional intelligence.
	Proximity: Proximity to place Lekki is a n added advantage(e.g., Ikate, Jakande, Agungi, Marwa, Victoria Island) to ensure punctual daily attendance.





Salary & Benefits


	Salary Range: N120,000 monthly
	Work Schedule: Monday – Friday (9:00 AM – 6:00 PM).
	Benefits: Paid time off, health insurance (HMO), and opportunities for career growth.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated CV and a professional cover letter to: [email protected] and copy: [email protected] using "Admin Assistant / Front Desk - Lekki" as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Job Vacancies at Beta Glass
content:
Beta Glass, a proud member of the Frigoglass Group, stands as a strategic partner to international beverage bottlers. Positioned as a major supplier of glass packaging, we play a crucial role in the burgeoning markets of West and Central Africa.

Our diverse range of glass containers offers superior packaging solutions to clients across various sectors, including soft drinks, beer, spirits, cosmetics, and pharmaceuticals. We are committed to delivering excellence in our products. At Beta Glass, we are not just suppliers; we are partners in progress, dedicated to setting new standards in the glass packaging industry.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) IT Administrator
     

            
    



Job Location: Agbara, Ogun

Job Purpose


	The IT Administrator is responsible for monitoring IT operations to ensure that enterprise networks and security systems perform at optimal levels. The role evaluates, recommends and leads the implementation of networking and security solutions.


Responsibilities


	Manage, upgrade, and maintain IT hardware, servers, operating systems, and enterprise networks (LAN/WAN).
	Ensure continuous, reliable network operations and monitor fire and access control systems.
	Document system configurations, changes, and procedures.
	Administer Microsoft 365 user accounts, permissions, and cloud storage provisioning.
	Ensure security, availability, and optimal use of cloud-based systems and services. Monitor and support Microsoft Dynamics NAV and enterprise applications.
	Manage and administer Microsoft SQL Server 2019 to support database operations.
	Deploy and manage security tools including BitLocker, VPNs, identity agents, and antivirus solutions
	Monitor systems for security breaches, review logs, and perform regular security scans
	Provide technical and backend support to business units, Helpdesk teams and IT Administrators.
	Monitor and resolve ServiceDesk (jira) tickets, providing remote and on-site support  


Qualifications & Experience


	Bachelor's Degree or Higher National Diploma (HND) in Computer Science, Information Technology, or any other related fields
	4 - 6 years of experience in a similar role
	Strong expertise in Windows Server and workstation operating systems, Microsoft 365 and server environments
	Microsoft Certified Professional (Solution Expert, Associate)
	Advanced knowledge of active Directory (Azure AD, and On-Prem), DNS, SSO, AD Certificate Services and windows-based management services
	Linux Certified Administrator, CompTIA, Networking or ITIL, CCNA will be an added advantage
	Good communication and documentation skills
	Professional interpersonal skills with the ability to collaborate across all levels of the organisation and within cross-functional teams.


Application Closing Date: 29th June, 2026.

Click here to apply online

 

2.) Warehouse Specialist

Job Location: Delta

Job Purpose


	This role is responsible for effective receipts, storage, control and issuance of engineering, raw and packaging materials required to meet production target.


Responsibilities


	Maintain existing layout & space management; effective warehouse keeping; work organization chart; procedures such as SOP as applicable.
	Manage stock control: the self-assured receipt, storage, retrieval and timely delivery of goods; transferring; document recording and data entry into system.
	Planning, Organizing and Controlling of store overall operations, maintaining end-to-end activities with all user departments.
	Put adequate measures and controls in place to ensure uninterrupted unloading of raw materials into Raw Materials Store.
	Coordinate the use of automated and computerized systems within the section and responds to and deals with customer communication by email, fax and telephone as applicable.
	Ensure issuance of materials by rotating inventory (FIFO) to avoid obsolescence, expiry and loss of company resources.
	Maintain an up-to-date register and ensure tracker for materials on ‘returnable and non-returnable basis
	Ensure 100% shelf visibility and rack compliance by good manufacturing practice.
	Ensure prompt documentation of materials/items receipt by generating SRVs and dispatch same with suppliers' invoice(s) to Finance accordingly.
	Issue Inventory in line with policies and procedures. Report items receipts without LPOs, Obsolete Materials Report, dead stock report, Materials Consumption report, OVP Delivery Report, Materials Expiry Report.


Qualifications


	Bachelor's Degree or Higher National Diploma (HND) in Logistics, Business Administration, Supply Chain Management or any other related fields.


The Person Must:


	Have sound knowledge of Warehouse and Distribution
	Have good communication skills - oral and written
	Be a Female for gender balance.


Experience:


	1- 3 years of experience in a similar role
	Knowledge of Microsoft Dynamics Navision or any other Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) software.


Application Closing Date: 16th July, 2026.

Click here to apply online

 

3.) Internal Auditor - Operational and IT

Job Location: Lagos

Job Purpose


	To execute risk-based operational and IT-related audit assignments across the business, including procurement, supply chain, manufacturing controls, logistics, user access, IT general controls, and selected investigations.
	The role supports the Operational Audit stream and provides embedded IT audit capability within the Internal Audit Function.


Key Responsibilities


	Participate in planning and execution of operational, compliance, and IT-related audits in line with the approved audit plan.
	Evaluate the adequacy and effectiveness of operational controls across procurement, inventory, manufacturing, logistics, sales-support processes, and related business activities.
	Perform IT-related audit procedures covering user access, IT general controls, change management, backup processes, and basic cybersecurity/control hygiene reviews.
	Conduct walkthroughs, control testing, data interrogation, and exception analysis using ERP and process data where available.
	Support investigations, fraud-risk reviews, and special assignments involving system access, operational anomalies, or process irregularities.
	Track implementation of agreed actions and support continuous monitoring and targeted follow-up reviews.
	Maintain and develop capability in Information Technology General Control (ITGC), ERP/process data analysis, and operational risk assessment.


Qualifications


	Bachelor's Degree or Higher National Diploma (HND) in Accounting, Information Systems, Computer Science, Engineering, Finance, or a related field
	CIA/ CISA/ CRISC/ ACA/ ACCA or related professional qualification is required


The Person Must:


	Have IT Audit & Systems Control Knowledge
	Have sound understanding of Operational Risk & Control Awareness


Experience:


	Must have 4-6 years of relevant audit experience in internal audit, operational audit, IT audit, external audit, or internal control review
	CRMA/ CFE/ ISO 9001 Lead Auditor and/or ISO 45001 Lead Auditor is desirable
	ITGC testing, ERP controls review, user access review, data analysis, or cybersecurity/control assessment exposure
	Knowledge of Microsoft Dynamics Navision or any other Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) software


Application Closing Date: 8th July, 2026.

Click here to apply online

     

    

        Click here to Apply

Site Engineer Job at Sow Real Estate Limited




        
    

        
        Sow Real Estate is a world-class luxury real estate super brand, with its core interest in the delivery of mass housing and luxury apartments. Sow is renowned for its sales, marketing and property management whilst emphasizing on the highest levels of excellence and Luxury. It is our mandate to systematically provide structure and infrastructure reflective of today’s technological advancement to meet the basic needs of housing. With perfection, detail and superior quality exhibited in all our projects, owning a Sow property is a certainly a living experience worth having.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Site Engineer
     

            
    



Job Location: Maitama, Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are currently seeking qualified and experienced Engineers to join the SOW Real Estate Team


Responsibilities


	Support and oversee land development and real estate construction projects from planning to execution.
	Prepare and review detailed construction drawings, specifications, and development permit documentation.
	Provide engineering and technical guidance to ensure adherence to best practices in project implementation.
	Coordinate and collaborate with multidisciplinary teams including architects, engineers, and other construction professionals.
	Monitor project progress, ensure quality delivery, manage site equipment and funds, and provide regular reports with visual updates.


Requirements


	3–5 years of relevant experience in construction or land development projects.
	Must be COREN registered (Council for the Regulation of Engineering in Nigeria).


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "SITE ENGINEER" as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Data Engineer",
  "description": "Babban Gona Agricultural Franchise is a financially sustainable, high impact and highly scalable social enterprise, which is part-owned by the farmers it serves. Babban Gona is an innovative model designed to serve the bottom of the pyramid and transform agriculture into a job-creation engine. Through this agricultural franchise, Babban Gona offers a suite of services to smallholder farmers, helping them to overcome the challenges of fragmentation and low economies of scale.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Data Engineer

Job Location: Lagos

Job Description
Why you matter:


	As we scale rapidly across geographies and platforms, the volume, complexity, and importance of our data continue to grow. With that growth comes the need for strong, reliable data foundations that support decision-making, regulatory needs, and operational excellence.
	We need a Data Engineer who can design and scale data systems thoughtfully&mdash;building pipelines and architectures that turn data into a strategic asset, not a bottleneck.


Responsibilities
You will:


	Design and maintain scalable, reliable data pipelines across platforms
	Ensure data quality, consistency, and accessibility across teams
	Support analytics, reporting, and decision-making with trusted datasets
	Partner with product, operations, and leadership to translate data needs into solutions
	Implement data governance, documentation, and best practices to support long-term scale
	Optimize data workflows and performance as data volumes and use cases grow
	You&rsquo;re not just moving data. You&rsquo;re building confidence in our numbers, enabling smarter decisions, and helping Babban Gona scale with clarity and impact.


Requirements


	You&rsquo;re the kind of person who sees patterns before they become problems.
	You think in systems, care deeply about quality, and understand that good decisions start with good data.
	You enjoy building for scale, but never lose sight of the people and impact behind the numbers.


You bring:


	2&ndash;4 years of hands-on experience as a Data Engineer or in a similar data-focused role
	Strong proficiency in SQL and Python for data transformation and pipeline development
	Experience building, maintaining, and optimizing data pipelines and data models
	A track record of working with cross-functional teams to deliver trusted datasets
	Clear communication skills&mdash;able to explain data concepts to non-technical stakeholders


Bonus if you have:


	Experience with cloud data platforms or warehouses
	Familiarity with data orchestration, data quality frameworks, or analytics engineering
	Experience working with growing or impact-driven organizations


You&rsquo;ll thrive if you&rsquo;re:


	Both thoughtful and hands-on&mdash;comfortable designing systems and getting into the details
	Curious, structured, and proactive about improving data reliability
	Collaborative&mdash;working closely with analytics, product, and operations teams
	Motivated by purpose and scale, knowing that at Babban Gona, better data directly enables better live.

Data Engineer Job at Babban Gona Agricultural Franchise




        
    

        
        Babban Gona Agricultural Franchise is a financially sustainable, high impact and highly scalable social enterprise, which is part-owned by the farmers it serves. Babban Gona is an innovative model designed to serve the bottom of the pyramid and transform agriculture into a job-creation engine. Through this agricultural franchise, Babban Gona offers a suite of services to smallholder farmers, helping them to overcome the challenges of fragmentation and low economies of scale.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Data Engineer

Job Location: Lagos

Job Description
Why you matter:
     

            
    




	As we scale rapidly across geographies and platforms, the volume, complexity, and importance of our data continue to grow. With that growth comes the need for strong, reliable data foundations that support decision-making, regulatory needs, and operational excellence.
	We need a Data Engineer who can design and scale data systems thoughtfully—building pipelines and architectures that turn data into a strategic asset, not a bottleneck.


Responsibilities
You will:


	Design and maintain scalable, reliable data pipelines across platforms
	Ensure data quality, consistency, and accessibility across teams
	Support analytics, reporting, and decision-making with trusted datasets
	Partner with product, operations, and leadership to translate data needs into solutions
	Implement data governance, documentation, and best practices to support long-term scale
	Optimize data workflows and performance as data volumes and use cases grow
	You’re not just moving data. You’re building confidence in our numbers, enabling smarter decisions, and helping Babban Gona scale with clarity and impact.


Requirements


	You’re the kind of person who sees patterns before they become problems.
	You think in systems, care deeply about quality, and understand that good decisions start with good data.
	You enjoy building for scale, but never lose sight of the people and impact behind the numbers.


You bring:


	2–4 years of hands-on experience as a Data Engineer or in a similar data-focused role
	Strong proficiency in SQL and Python for data transformation and pipeline development
	Experience building, maintaining, and optimizing data pipelines and data models
	A track record of working with cross-functional teams to deliver trusted datasets
	Clear communication skills—able to explain data concepts to non-technical stakeholders


Bonus if you have:


	Experience with cloud data platforms or warehouses
	Familiarity with data orchestration, data quality frameworks, or analytics engineering
	Experience working with growing or impact-driven organizations


You’ll thrive if you’re:


	Both thoughtful and hands-on—comfortable designing systems and getting into the details
	Curious, structured, and proactive about improving data reliability
	Collaborative—working closely with analytics, product, and operations teams
	Motivated by purpose and scale, knowing that at Babban Gona, better data directly enables better live.
    

    

        Click here to Apply
